elements.sent-2411-001: In this paper, we consider a modified version of Smirnov operator and obtain some Bernstein-type inequalities preserved by this operator.
elements.sent-2411-002: In particular, we prove some compact generalizations of the well-known inequalities of Bernstein, Erd\"{o s and Lax, Ankeny and Rivlin and others.}
elements.sent-2411-003: Key words and phrases: Modified Smirnov operator, Polynomials, Bernstein inequality, Restricted zeros.
elements.sent-2411-004: Let $\mathbb{P _{n}$ denote the class of polynomials $P(z):=\sum_{j=0 ^{n a_jz^j$ in $\mathbb{C}$ of degree at most $n \in \mathbb{N}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-005: Let $\mathbb{D}$ be the open unit disk $\left\{z \in \mathbb{C ;|z|<1\right\}$, such that $\overline{\mathbb{D }$ is the closure of $\mathbb{D}$ and $B(\mathbb{D )$ denotes its boundary.}}}
elements.sent-2411-006: Let $P \in \mathbb{P _{n}$, then \begin{eqnarray}\label{1} \max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P^{\prime}(z)|\le n \max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(z)|\end{eqnarray} and \begin{eqnarray}\label{2} \max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(Rz)|\le R^{n} \max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(z)|.\end{eqnarray}.}
elements.sent-2411-007: Inequality (\ref{1 ) is a well-known theorem of Bernstein \cite{SB .}}
elements.sent-2411-008: The inequality (\ref{2 ) is a simple deduction from the maximum modulus principle.}
elements.sent-2411-009: In both the inequalities, the equality holds for $P(z)=\alpha{z^{n , \alpha\ne 0$.}}
elements.sent-2411-010: If we restrict to a class of polynomials having no zeros in $\mathbb{D}$, the inequalities (\ref{1 ) and (\ref{2 ) can be sharpened.}}
elements.sent-2411-011: In fact, if $P(z)\ne 0$ in $\mathbb{D}$, then \begin{eqnarray}\label{3} \max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P^{\prime}(z)|\le \frac{n}{2} \max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(z)|\end{eqnarray} and for $R>1,$ \begin{eqnarray}\label{4} \max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(Rz)|\le \frac{R^{n}+1}{2} \max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(z)|.\end{eqnarray}.
elements.sent-2411-012: Inequality (\ref{3 ) was proved by Erd\"{o s and Lax \cite{EL8 , whereas Ankeny and Rivlin \cite{AR1 used (\ref{3 ) to prove (\ref{4 ).}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-013: These inequalities were further improved by Aziz and Dawood \cite{AD2 , where under the same hypothesis, it was proved that \begin{eqnarray}\max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P^{\prime}(z)|\le \frac{n}{2}\left\{\max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(z)|- \min_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(z)|\right\}\end{eqnarray} and for $R>1$ \begin{equation}\label{6} \max_{z\in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(Rz)|\le \left\{\frac{R^n+1}{2}\right\}\max_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(z)|-\left\{\frac{R^n-1}{2}\right\} \min_{z \in B(\mathbb{D})}|P(z)|.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-014: The equality in (\ref{3 )-(\ref{6 ) holds for the polynomials of the form $P(z)=\alpha {z ^n+\beta$, with $|\alpha|=|\beta|$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-015: In 1930 Bernstein \cite{BB4 also proved the following result:.}

elements.sent-2411-024: The modified Smirnov operator $\tilde{\mathbb{S _{a}$ is more preferred in a sense than Smirnov operator $\mathbb{S _{\alpha}$, because the parameter $a$ of $\tilde{\mathbb{S _{a}$ does not depend on $z$ unlike parameter $\alpha$ of $\mathbb{S _{\alpha}$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-025: Marden \cite{MM introduced a differential operator $\mathbb{B : \mathbb{P _{n \to \mathbb{P _{n}$ of $mth$ order.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-026: This operator carries a polynomial $p \in \mathbb{P _{n}$ into \begin{eqnarray}\mathbb{B}(z)=\lambda_{0} p(z)+ \lambda_{1} \frac{nz}{2}p^{\prime}(z)+...+\lambda_{m} \left(\frac{nz}{2}\right)^m p^{m}(z),\end{eqnarray} where $\lambda_{0 , \lambda_{1 ,...,\lambda_{m}$ are constants such that \begin{eqnarray}\label{8} u(z)=\lambda_{0} +\binom{n}{1} \lambda_{1}z +...+ \binom{n}{m} \lambda_{m} z^m\ne0,\quad for \quad Re(z)>\frac{n}{4}.\end{eqnarray}.}}}
elements.sent-2411-027: Rahman and Schmeisser \cite{QR considered the Marden operator for $m=2$ and showed that this operator preserves the inequalities between polynomials and accordingly proved the following:.}
elements.sent-2411-028: Let $p$ and $P$ be polynomials possessing conditions as in Theorem \ref{Theorem 1.1 , Then \begin{eqnarray}\label{9} |\mathbb{B}(z)| \le |\mathbb{B}[P](z)|\quad for \quad z \in \mathbb{C}\setminus{\mathbb{D}},\end{eqnarray} where the constants $\lambda_{0 , \lambda_{1 ,...,\lambda_{m}$ possess condition (\ref{8 ).}}}}
elements.sent-2411-029: For $z\in{\mathbb{C}\setminus{\mathbb{D }$ in (\ref{9 ), the equality holds if and only if $p(z) = \gamma z^n,~\gamma \ne0$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-030: A variety of key papers concerning the $\mathbb{B}$-operator have appeared in the literature \cite{IQ, WL .}
elements.sent-2411-031: In order to compare the Smirnov operator $\mathbb{S _{\alpha (z):=zp^{\prime (z)-n\alpha p(z)$ and the Rahman’s operator (with $\lambda_{2 = 0$) $\mathbb{B (z)=\lambda_{0 p(z)+ \lambda_{1 \frac{nz {2 p^{\prime (z)$, we require $\alpha \in \overline{\Omega _{|z|}$ in inequality (\ref{7 ) and in inequality (\ref{9 ) the root of the polynomial $u(z) = \lambda_{0 + n \lambda_{1 z$ should lie in the half-plane $Re(z) \le \frac{n {4}$, that is \begin{eqnarray}Re\left(-\frac{\lambda_{0}}{\lambda_{1}n}\right) \le \frac{n}{4}.\end{eqnarray}.}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}

elements.sent-2411-037: These facts were first observed by Ganenkova and Starkov \cite{GS6 .}
elements.sent-2411-038: In this paper, we prove some more general results concerning the modified Smirnov operator preserving inequalities between polynomials, which in turn yields compact generalizations of some well-known polynomial inequalities.
elements.sent-2411-039: Before writing our main results, we prove the following lemmas which are required for their proofs.
